src_model_base_v1.note: Transparent scenario model. Not a claimed Waymo or Tesla P&L.

src_cost_architecture.citation: Robotaxi Scalability High-Confidence Consolidated Research v1
src_cost_architecture.note: Supports the three-layer taxonomy, four scalability mechanisms and double-counting controls.

src_depreciation_residual.citation: Consolidated Robotaxi Depreciation and Residual Value Research v1
src_depreciation_residual.note: Supports scenario treatment of lifetime and residual value. No mature secondary market for purpose-built robotaxis is established.

src_utilisation.citation: Sprint 2 Consolidation: Utilisation, Empty Miles and Paid-Mile Ratio
src_utilisation.note: Supports the 0.55 Waymo-like base paid-mile ratio and scenario ranges.

src_energy_depot.citation: Sprint 3 Consolidation: Energy, Charging, Depot and Infrastructure
src_energy_depot.note: Supports direct running-cost and physical-enablement ranges.

src_ops_labour.note: Supports local fleet-operations categories; exact staffing and cost remain scenario-based.

src_tesla_consensus.citation: Tesla Robotaxi and Cybercab Research Consensus v1
src_tesla_consensus.note: High confidence in architectural differences, medium in direction of savings, low in realised economics.

src_uber_service_fee.note: Uber states the service fee varies by trip and is the residual after driver earnings, third-party fees and operational expenses.

src_lyft_fee_cap.citation: Lyft, The Lyft fee is capped every month
src_lyft_fee_cap.note: Lyft states its fee averages approximately 14% and is capped at 30% of passenger payments monthly; external fees are separate.

src_lyft_earnings.citation: Lyft, The driver's guide to pay
src_lyft_earnings.note: Lyft describes a 30% platform-fee cap before external fees.

src_tesla_robotaxi_support.note: Confirms current service areas, operating hours, Model Y fleet and direct Tesla app.

src_tesla_q1_2026.note: States expected volume production of Cybercab in 2026. Does not disclose realised unit cost.

src_tesla_maps_job.citation: Tesla Careers, Machine Learning Engineer, Maps & Navigation, Robotaxi
src_tesla_maps_job.note: Confirms Tesla develops accurate road-network maps for Robotaxi and vehicle navigation; Tesla is map-light, not map-free.

src_tesla_cybercab_claims.citation: Tesla We, Robot Cybercab claims, October 2024
src_tesla_cybercab_claims.note: Used only to document future Cybercab claims. The sub-$30,000 vehicle and low operating-cost figures are targets, not realised economics.

src_waymo_current_service_2026.citation: Waymo, Rides: Serving Riders In, accessed June 2026
src_waymo_current_service_2026.note: Waymo lists 11 current rider-service markets: Atlanta, Austin, Dallas, Houston, Los Angeles, Miami, Nashville, Orlando, Phoenix, San Antonio and the San Francisco Bay Area.

src_waymo_nhtsa_3791.citation: NHTSA Recall Campaign 26E026, Waymo 5th and 6th Generation ADS, May 2026
src_waymo_nhtsa_3791.note: Potential number of affected ADS units is 3,791. This is an installed affected-unit count, not a confirmed active commercial fleet count.

src_waymo_weekly_miles_2026.note: Waymo states it is driving over 4 million miles weekly. Used only to triangulate an active-fleet-equivalent range under the model's 70,000-mile annual assumption.

src_waymo_expansion_2026.citation: Waymo, 2025 Year in Review and 2026 city expansion plans
src_waymo_expansion_2026.note: Waymo said it was laying groundwork for ride-hailing operations in over 20 additional cities during 2026. A 25-city high scenario is therefore conservative rather than aggressive.
